title: Salsa Verde (Green Sauce)

ingredients.title: Ingredients (serves 6–8)
items: 2–3 serrano chiles (or jalapeños for milder heat)
1 tsp sea salt, adjust to taste
2–3 tbsp neutral oil (vegetable or avocado), optional for silky texture

instruction.title: Roast or Boil the Tomatillos & Chilies
steps: Roasting: Cook tomatillos, chiles, onion, and garlic on a hot skillet until lightly charred (5–8 min per side).
Boiling: Boil tomatillos and chilies for 8–10 minutes until softened.

instruction.title: Blend the Sauce
steps: Add tomatillos, chiles, onion, garlic, cilantro, salt, and pepper to blender.
Blend until smooth and adjust seasoning with lime juice or sugar.

instruction.title: Refine Texture (Optional)
steps: Strain through fine mesh sieve for smooth texture.
Whisk in oil slowly for a richer, silky sauce.

instruction.title: Serve or Store
steps: Serve with tacos, eggs, or grilled meats.
Store in fridge up to 5 days or freeze up to 2 months.

chefTips: Choose firm, bright green tomatillos for best flavor.
Roasting reduces acidity and adds depth.
Serrano chiles increase heat; remove seeds for milder version.
Add cilantro at the end to preserve color and freshness.
Oil makes the sauce more restaurant-style and smooth.
